key_insight: A two-stage parametric finite element scheme can simultaneously enforce Helfrich-flow energy dissipation, exact volume/surface-area conservation, and good mesh quality for evolving curves and surfaces.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical structure-preserving discretization for membrane and interface simulations where geometric constraints and numerical stability are critical.
method: Stage 1: solve a linearized curvature-evolution step to compute normal velocity with soft volume/area constraints.
Stage 2: update the mesh using the computed normal velocity plus a BGN-type tangential velocity.
Apply a time-weighted interface normal and an area-correction multiplier; solve the resulting nonlinear correction with Newton iteration.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on 2D curves and 3D surfaces, including nonsmooth initial data and nonzero spontaneous curvature, to validate accuracy and robustness.
watch_item: The exact conservation step is nonlinear and depends on Newton solves, so robustness and cost may be sensitive to timestep choice and convergence behavior.
paper_title: Geometric structure-preserving parametric finite element approximations for the constrained Helfrich flow
